scenario: A local OpenClaw gateway user can load the plugin and call loo_* tools to search, describe, expand, dry-run, inspect cockpit cards, and reason about operating attention from bounded summaries without raw transcript access.
expected_public_safe_evidence: packaged agent usage skill path
plugin loaded status
declared loo_* tool names
bounded index counts
search refs and score summaries
describe and direct-session/query expand profile names
recent session and cockpit inbox card counts
active-scope recent session ordering that demotes stale low-confidence blocked residue below current approval, running, and waiting lanes
collaboration cockpit active-lane counts, attention levels, Desktop state boundary, source coverage, and false action flags
clean card title/objective/summary/nextAction fields plus presentation_cleaned or presentation_low_confidence reason codes when extraction needed cleanup
watcher status, watcher_triggered reason codes, resume request packet expiry, mutates=false, and approval boundary
persisted watcher observation refs, attention queue refs, watcher source coverage, execute=false queue tool calls, and false action flags
Codex app-server read-only availability, allowed/forbidden method posture, thread source coverage, and no-turns proof
visible Codex map source coverage, confidence, ambiguity markers, and public-safe session card refs
Codex Desktop coherence state distinguishing cli_visible from desktop_visible, desktop_refresh_required, desktop_restart_required, and unknown proof states
Codex Desktop fallback readiness with CUA-first and Peekaboo-secondary blocker codes, focus status, visible-screen warning, and coherence_input_missing next-tool guidance when coherence is omitted
GitHub operating item normalization for issue, PR, statusCheckRollup, and check records without raw bodies or logs
operating-picture source coverage, source authority coverage, current_lane/fresh_signal/low_confidence_downgraded ranking reason codes, and not_configured P1 source states

end-to-end Eva cockpit dogfood evidence tying GitHub check state, recent Codex cards, clean presentation, current-lane ranking, customer/runtime/security priority, source coverage, and P1 not_configured gaps into one public-safe workflow
dry-run approval_audit_id, params_hash, and message_hash
gateway setupStatus, setupBlockers, and setupGuidance for first-run credential blockers
agent reasoning note based only on safe summaries
collaboration next-step planner schema, status counts, execute=false tool packets, blockers, reason codes, and false action flags
exact read-only next tool calls for watcher resume packets, Desktop coherence, and fallback status without executing them
action-bound Codex Desktop collaboration proof report with target ref, backend, target app/window, action hash, approval freshness, source coverage, execute=false next packet, and false action flags
runtime Desktop visibility status report with covered, partial, and blocked lane counts, source coverage, read-only execute=false next proof calls, and false action flags

active-thread state report with running, blocked, needs-nudge, stale, waiting, approval-needed, idle, unknown, confidence, freshness, reason codes, source coverage, attention coverage, non-executed read-only probe recommendations, non-executed control dry-run recommendations, and false action flags

Codex autonomy tick report with prioritized execute=false read-only probes and control dry-run recommendations, idempotency keys, stop conditions, approval boundaries, source coverage, and false action flags
prepared-state status, prepared card refs, prepared inbox refs, source coverage, authority coverage, stale/partial/unknown reason codes, execute=false actions, and bounded summary-leaf expansion refs
pass_criteria: The npm package includes an agent-facing skill/playbook for the canonical staged recall and dry-run workflows.
OpenClaw gateway can discover the plugin or the issue records why the gateway is unavailable.
Agent can search sessions through loo_* read tools and receive source-prefixed refs.
Agent can describe and expand selected refs within a bounded token profile.
Agent can list recent session cards and a deterministic cockpit inbox without a wildcard query.
Agent can call active-scope recent sessions and see stale low-confidence blocked residue explicitly marked and ranked below current approval, running, and waiting work.
Agent can inspect one read-only collaboration cockpit summary that composes recent cards, inbox urgency, watcher requests, and supplied Desktop coherence/fallback evidence without performing actions.
Agent-facing card summaries and next actions are cleaned deterministic presentation text rather than raw directive fragments, markdown tables, or duplicated Title/Final labels.
Agent can inspect read-only watcher status and generate resume-request packets without live control, GUI mutation, cleanup, or external writes.
Agent can inspect persisted watcher observations and local attention queue items; every queued tool call remains `execute:false`, source-ref-backed, and public-safe.
Agent can inspect Codex app-server status and thread signals without reading raw turns, enabling remote control, or mutating Codex.
Agent can correlate sanitized visible Codex candidates with indexed session cards while ambiguous title matches stay low confidence.
Agent can classify Codex Desktop coherence without treating CLI/direct/app-server visibility as Desktop-visible collaboration proof.

Agent can inspect CUA/Peekaboo fallback readiness and blocker codes without triggering GUI mutation, screenshots, refresh, restart, click, select, type, or live Codex control; if coherence is omitted for a target, the report returns coherence_input_missing with the exact loo_codex_desktop_coherence args to run first.
Agent can normalize public-safe GitHub issue, PR, statusCheckRollup, and check records into digest-ready github_items before summarization.
Agent can distinguish pending, failed, passed, and unknown PR check-state reason codes without reading check logs.
Agent can see current-lane GitHub PR/check signals rank above old low-confidence Codex cards unless customer/runtime/security impact is present.
Agent can run or inspect the Eva operating-picture dogfood scenario as one coherent workflow instead of trusting isolated ranking, check-state, and card-cleanup tests.
Agent can inspect operating-picture cards whose claims cite source refs, confidence, freshness, reason codes, and authority coverage.
Missing P1 source adapters are reported as not_configured instead of summarized.
Unavailable or cache-only authorities degrade to unknown or low confidence instead of being treated as current truth.
Agent can dry-run a control action and reason about the audit id without sending a live action.
Evidence contains public-safe summaries, counts, refs, hashes, setupStatus classifications, and blocker codes only.
Agent can ask `loo_codex_collaboration_next_steps` for exact execute=false next tool packets after reading the cockpit, instead of inferring tool order from prose.
Planner output blocks fallback/action lanes that need approval or missing proof instead of claiming live Desktop collaboration.

Agent can validate an exact Codex Desktop collaboration proof packet through `loo_codex_desktop_collaboration_proof`; generic click/type/send/continue requests fail closed and the returned next tool call is execute=false.

Agent can ask `loo_codex_runtime_desktop_visibility_status` for one compact covered/partial/blocked status across active Desktop collaboration lanes, with source coverage and next proof calls still read-only and execute=false.

Agent can ask `loo_codex_active_thread_state` for one compact active-thread state answer; each item reports attention coverage and any next read-only probe with `execute:false`; conflicting app-server/indexed signals degrade to unknown or low confidence instead of being hidden or overclaimed; and needs-nudge lanes can expose a `nextControlDryRun` packet with `execute:false` instead of making the agent invent control args.

Agent can ask `loo_codex_autonomy_tick` for one deterministic ordered loop tick whose steps are all `execute:false`, put read-only probes before control dry-run recommendations for the same lane, include idempotency keys and stop conditions, and preserve approval boundaries without minting audit ids or performing actions.

Agent can ask `loo_prepared_state_status`, `loo_prepared_cards`, and `loo_prepared_inbox` for advisory prepared Codex state; cards and inbox items cite opaque refs, source coverage, authority coverage, freshness, confidence, stale/partial/unknown reason codes, and any next action remains `execute:false`.
fail_criteria: Agent must read raw transcripts to answer the session-management question.
Gateway exposes live mutation without dry-run approval proof.
Evidence captures local DBs, transcript files, screenshots, prompt text, or secrets.
Tool output is too vague for the agent to choose a safe next action.
Card summaries or next actions expose directive syntax, markdown tables, duplicated Title/Final prefixes, or raw transcript-shaped excerpts as user-facing presentation.
Watcher event reads or local attention queue items expose raw transcript paths, raw prompt spans, raw tool payloads, secrets, or executable live-control/GUI actions.
Prepared cards or inbox items expose raw transcript paths, raw prompt spans, raw tool payloads, secrets, or executable live-control/GUI actions.
Operating-picture output hides missing sources, omits authority coverage, treats readable empty PLAN_STATE input as not configured, or treats PLAN_STATE prose as current truth.
known_gaps: Fresh OpenClaw profiles need scoped gateway credentials or env-ref onboarding before tool-smoke can call tools; clean-profile proof must stay separate from already-provisioned local gateway proof.

P0 operating-picture tools use LCO/Codex, normalized public-safe GitHub inputs, explicit PLAN_STATE pins, read-only watcher request packets, and a public source-authority profile only; Notion, support-control, Company Brain, Stripe, dashboard/export, and summarizer adapters remain P1.
Visible Codex map joins prove read-only correlation from sanitized title/status metadata only; they do not prove Codex GUI mutation or visible focus/select/continue workflows.

Codex Desktop coherence classification can prove cli_visible, desktop_visible, desktop_refresh_required, desktop_restart_required, or unknown states from supplied public-safe evidence. Fallback status can report CUA/Peekaboo readiness, and when called with only a target it returns coherence_input_missing plus the exact coherence tool args instead of inventing hidden state. The collaboration cockpit can summarize those states across active lanes, the next-step planner can emit execute=false packets for the next safe proof step, the action-bound proof contract can validate exact dry-run approval packets, runtime visibility status can summarize covered/partial/blocked lane coverage, active-thread state can classify running/blocked/needs-nudge/stale lanes with conflict downgrades, attention coverage, non-executed read-only probe recommendations, and non-executed control dry-run recommendations, and autonomy tick can order those next probes/dry-run recommendations into one deterministic execute=false loop packet. None of these performs a live direct-protocol action, Desktop refresh/restart, or GUI mutation.
This does not prove live Codex control, generic GUI mutation, Claude Code adapter parity, full business truth, stable release, npm latest promotion, or release-grade customer readiness.
next_action: Continue LCO 1.2 prepared-state work with hook sidecar closeout, state-prep, and compaction-marker capture after watcher persistence lands.
proof_boundary: This proves local OpenClaw gateway agent usability for Codex read/search/describe/expand/detail lookup/recommend/dry-run, read-only watcher request packets, persisted watcher events, execute-false local attention queue items, read-only app-server status/thread signals, visible-to-indexed session correlation, Codex Desktop coherence classification, Codex Desktop fallback readiness reporting, read-only collaboration cockpit summaries, read-only execute=false collaboration next-step packets, action-bound dry-run Desktop collaboration proof contracts, runtime Desktop visibility status reporting, read-only active-thread state classification with attention coverage, non-executed read-only probe recommendations and control dry-run recommendations, deterministic execute=false autonomy tick planning, P0 cockpit/operating-picture cards, deterministic prepared-state cards and inbox items, bounded summary-leaf expansion, and fresh-profile gateway-ready invocation using public-safe evidence only; it does not prove live control, GUI mutation, unattended Desktop-visible live collaboration, Claude Code adapter parity, full business truth, stable release, npm latest promotion, or enterprise/customer readiness.
